[PUBLISHED DATE]: March 24, 2023
[TITLE]: Why tornadoes are still hard to forecast — even though storm predictions are improving
[METADATA/INTRO]: As a deadly tornado headed toward Rolling Fork, Mississippi, on March 24, 2023, forecasters saw the storm developing on radar and issued a rare “tornado emergency” warning. NOAA’s Weather Prediction and Storm Prediction centers had been warning for several days...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The vast majority of violent tornadoes form from supercells, thunderstorms with a deep rotating updraft, called a “mesocyclone.” Vertical wind shear can enable the midlevels of the storm to rotate, and upward suction from this mesocyclone can intensify the rotation within the storm’s outflow into a tornado. 
 If you have a supercell and it has strong rotation above the ground, that’s often a precursor to a tornado. Some research suggests that a wider mesocyclone is more likely to create a stronger, longer-lasting tornado than other storms. 
 Forecasters also look at the storm’s environmental conditions – temperature, humidity and wind shear. Those offer more clues that a storm is likely to produce a significant tornado.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: File:Evolution of a Tornado.jpg - Wikimedia Commons
[METADATA/INTRO]: File:Evolution of a Tornado.jpg Captions...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Français : Cette image a été créée à partir de huit prises en deux séquences d'une tornade qui s'est formée au nord de Minneola, au Kansas, le 24 mai 2016. Cette supercellule prolifique a produit au moins 12 tornades et parfois deux ou même trois tornades à la fois. 
 Magyar: Hét képből összeállított montázs egy tornádó kialakulásáról 2016. május 24-én (Észak-Minneola, Clark megye, Kansas, Amerikai Egyesült Államok) 
 Italiano: Immagine realizzata a partire da otto fotografie scattate in sequenza che ritrae la formazione di un tornado a nord di Minneola, in Kansas, il 24 maggio 2016 
 Македонски: Образување на торнадо во осум последователни слики споени во една. Сликано кај Минеола, Канзас, на 24 мај 2016 г.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2018-09-05
[TITLE]: This Picture Shows the Birth of a Tornado | PetaPixel
[METADATA/INTRO]: This Picture Shows the Birth of a Tornado Storm chasing photographer Jason Weingart shot a series of time-lapse photos of a tornado forming in Kansas back in 2016. Afterward, he selected eight of the frames and created this composite photo...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: This Picture Shows the Birth of a Tornado 
 Storm chasing photographer Jason Weingart shot a series of time-lapse photos of a tornado forming in Kansas back in 2016. Afterward, he selected eight of the frames and created this composite photo titled “Evolution of a Tornado.” 
 Weingart submitted the photo to the 2017 Wiki Science Competition and had it selected as a Jury’s Choice Winner. 
 Here’s the resulting timelapse video of the same tornado: 
 Despite clearly describing the image and how it was created, the picture later went viral online as people described it as a single photo showing multiple tornadoes side-by-side.
